text: When setting project goals, it’s important to use the SMART framework. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal would be “increase website traffic by 20% within three months.” Breaking the project into milestones helps you track progress, like completing a design phase or user testing. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, such as a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing these goals with everyone involved, from team members to clients. This way, all expectations are clear, and the project stays on track.
